review_text: My most favourite movie of all time!
What took me so long to watch this masterpiece!?!!! Top 5 war film I’ve seen. Brilliant all the way around.

Best War Drama, Best Thriller, Best EVERYTHING Movie. One of the few films that are actually better than the book. Recommend watching the longer Director’s Cut…settle in and take it all in. Stellar from start to finish.
An intense look at underwater warfare during WWII.

I’ve watched the director’s cut and it was absolutely amazing. It reminded me why older movies are so special they’re crafted with real passion, attention to detail, and atmosphere that pulls you in completely. Every scene felt alive, the acting was powerful, and the pacing kept me engaged from start to finish. The story had real depth and emotion, even if the ending felt a bit depressing. Still, that doesn’t take away from how incredible the whole experience was. I’d easily give it a solid 4.5/5 – nearly perfect in every aspect.
A Must-watch movie. Absolutely brilliant story, great actors and fabulous filmography. Start to End gritty, suspenseful and brilliant ending.

Coming from a current Submariner, Das Boot is the pinnacle of submarine movies. It might even be one of the best war movies ever made. You will not regret watching this. Recommend the 3 hour Directors Cut version. It builds each character to the point where they feel like your friends and you are part of the crew. No other submarine movie has ever made me feel that way, and this movie has one of the best endings I have ever seen in cinema. -ETV1(SS)
Absolutely The best submarine movie ever A Must Watch!

Filme fraco, o roteiro é fraco, as cenas são fracas, a história é fraca, o elenco é fraco, e ninguém ajuda a melhorar o filme, os personagens são fracos, e o filme deveria ter cenas bem melhores e relevantes, para fazer o filme ser bom, e o filme ficou muito cansativo com mais de 3 horas de duração.

A lot of war movies spend a lot of time saying “war is bad”. Das Boot instead says “humans are incredible, resilient, ingenious creatures, and war is the worst possible thing we can do with our talents”. The atmosphere is crushing but homey, with fruit and sausages dangling from steel pipes, a shared latrine for a crew of 50, and sopping wet, well, everything. This film uses the camera incredibly effectively, incorporating character close-ups, frantic sprinting shots, and foggy, underwater landscapes to seamlessly, almost unconsciously transition the perspective, connecting you with specific characters at times, and at others, making you feel like just another member of the crew. The set work is very much deserving of all the awards it received. Apart from an occasionally wonky green screen in some external shots (I mean, it was 1981, after all), this film could not feel more real if it was a documentary. And the soundtrack dates the film just a little, but it’s forgivable and maybe a little charming. The pacing of the story is unique, given to the fact that it’s really just a shortened cut of a 6-episode miniseries. But it works for a film of this length, feeling naturally episodic while still consistently building pressure (no pun intended). All in all, Das Boot is a feat of filmmaking and a great option for anyone who likes being stressed (in a good way).
Claustrophobic. Wolfgang Peterson captures the intensity of the situation in an incredible way. One of the best films of its kind.
Like any really good war movie, this shows the human toll & ignorance of war. Great writing, acting and special effects.

I saw this 3 times the first year it was out, 1981, in German with English subtitles. Then later they released it dubbed in English, and I saw it again. Very authentic looking scenes, big or small. Told from the German perspective, if you like war movies, this is as good as it gets, a masterpiece....and I need to see it again!
Faultless. OK the water effects may be improved now, but otherwise can't fault it fir drama, humour, tension, claustrophobia. Amazing film I can watch again and again.
Solid WW2 German sub adventure, With, you are there, intense realism, Strong characters, Shocking and sobering, Movie really hammers home the futility of war message.
10/10 absolutely brilliant.
Possibly the best war movie ever made. Simply breathtaking.
Quite simply one of the greatest movies ever made.
Fantastic movie! Very well made considering that it's from the 80's. Love it!!

DAS BOOT is top notch in ✨every✨ sense (especially for its early 80s production period). Some scenes are mesmerizing suspence and others are almost "jump outta your seat" intensity. Probably the most intense part about DAS BOOT is that the story's basis and its different scenes are based in something that actually did occur. GREAT flick...!!!

An all-time great movie. The realism and suspense are insane.
Hands down one of the best German movies ever done.

Can I give this film 6 stars. It has all the emotion, it has the action and it really brings the horrors of submarine warfare in World War 2 to life. Amazing film!
Top Film. Ein Muss für jeden.

Good film, cool experimental cinematography, although I think the film is a little overrated. The ending is a little contrived, can't they just kill some people anywhere in the film and just have the survivors reach the end? Also, I wished there were more peacetime scenes involving the crew to contrast with the grim place of submarine warfare. This film really needed a Eugene Sledge holding up a flower in front of the sun type post-war type of scene in it. And not enough eating/shitting/masturbation/casual chit chat scenes. There's also like one happy moment in this which lasts like 5 minutes long and it's a pyrrhic one. Goes to show that the anti-war genre can be a little contrived at times.
I can't believe I'd never seen this movie.
The claustrophobic nature of it's setting, combined with a taut script adds to the suspense in this gripping war movie. One of the best war movies ever made.
One of most powerful and cool movies about submarines
One of best war movies of all time . Gripping moving and never lets you fully relax. Great story with ups and downs of life on a German Uboat during WWII.
10/10. Best on your first watch, do not watch any material about the film beforehand.

A tense, claustrophobic, hard-hitting, and very unusual anti-war film following a Nazi submarine crew hunting British cargo ships in the Atlantic Ocean. You get to know every crew member, including the hulking metallic beast of a ship, as they are stretched to their limits. The interior claustrophobic scenes are nicely contrasted by the few exterior scenes that provide both relief and dismay. I saw the Director's Cut which, despite running over 3 hours, never felt slow or mis-paced. Deserves several re-watches!
One of the best, if not the best war movie I've ever seen. It's the reason I'll never set foot on a sub. The theatrical version is fantastic, the extended version even better.
One of, if not the best, war films of all time. Claustrophobic and dour, with explosions of excitement and fear.

The good thing about historical flicks is that they don't really age if they're done well. This is a prime example, one of the greatest movies I've ever seen. I recommend the Director's Cut over the TV series.
Best of the best in a tv serie...probaley

"Das Boot" is easily among the most immersive war films ever made. This movie threw me into a situation that is intense and relentless the whole way through. I watched the director's cut which is a whole hour longer, but I felt the experience felt good even though it was long. Extending the chaos works for the feeling the film intends to create. The movie can be very chaotic at times, but also that is part of the point, it can just make it a bit exhausting to watch. Overall, this is an older war film that ages well on a technical level and shows a glimpse of a perspective that many films shy away from, which makes it a refreshing watch in the genre.

I watched the 208 minute Director's Cut of the film and while long, it really holds your attention and is a great look into the opposing side's view and adversities in the time of war. The film really does a great job of conveying the struggles of both sides in war, nobody wins, we all lose no matter the outcome. The submarine is both so powerful and stealthy but is also so fragile as well, any damage and the impact is devastating to the crew on board. We see how claustrophobic and terrifying it can be when you don't know at any time if you will be hit or the submarine damaged enough to give way to the enormous pressure on it by the ocean. The films conveys this haunting and tight interior and suspense is teeming from beginning to end in this great and meaningful story of war.

Über vierzig Jahre alt, aber auch heute noch mitreisend. Einer der besten Kriegsfilme. Der Film basiert auf den gleichnamigen Roman von Lothar-Günther Buchheim, das Drehbuch schrieb und Regie führte Wolfgang Petersen. Toller Antikriegsfilm mit den besten Darstellern.

One of the only films I've ever watched where I felt like I was there. Cramped up inside a submarine. There are no borders or nations in this film. Only sense of impending doom and futility of war. An ending which is forever etched in my memory
Possibly the best non Hollywood war movie of all time. It goes deep if you know what I mean.

An absolutely jarring and beautiful film which takes the viewer through the depths of the human mind, and back again, and then back, and back again... Treat yourself to the 3hr+ version of the film or even better the almost 5 hour long miniseries. You can't go wrong.

Having been born in 1996 with a cursory understanding of the politics and reality of World War II, no movie has gripped me with the general dilemma of an axis military member; land, air, or sea. The pride, trial and eventual listless disposition felt direct, painful and crudely forward with the grim realities of life aboard a German U-boat following to occupation of France. Must see for any professional or self proclaimed afficianado of World War II or subnautical history. This film is both hard to swallow and impossible to look away. The daunting 3.5 hour runtime should not be a deterrrant, this film will have you at the edge of your seat. Must see. //Rough google translate: "War is hell for those who have not tried it" "Krieg ist die Hölle für diejenigen, die es nicht versucht haben" The irony is not lost on me. Watch this film

This film is very long but stick with it as it's worth the watch it's about a u boat crew and what life on a u-boat is like from the engineer having a break down and the captain is not happy about it and gets his gun to shoot him but the crew drags him away before he can do anything and him telling a reporter who is on the u-boat to not to take photos of them clean shaven but to take photos with them with beards because they will then look like men there's a lot more else I could say but I won't just watch and be engrossed
